file_read.description: Read the contents of a file in the workspace.

input.path: string — relative path inside workspace, e.g. 'src/main.py'

file_read.returns: { status, path, content, lines } or { status, error }

file_write.description: Write or create a file in the workspace. Creates parent directories automatically.

input.content: string — full file content to write
input.overwrite: bool (optional, default true) — set false to prevent overwriting

file_write.returns: { status, path, bytes } or { status, error }

file_list.description: List files and directories inside a workspace folder.

input.path: string — relative path to list, use '.' for workspace root

file_list.returns: { status, path, entries: [{ name, type, size }] }

file_delete.description: Delete a single file from the workspace. Cannot delete directories.

input.path: string — relative path to the file

code_run.description: PREFERRED tool for running scripts. Use 'inputs' list to pass stdin — never use shell piping for this. Supports .py, .js, .ts, .sh, .rb files.

input.inputs: list of strings — stdin lines in order. For 3 input() calls pass ['10', '20', '30']

code_run.returns: { status, exit_code, stdout, stderr } or { status, error }

shell_exec.description: Run a shell command. On Windows: use 'python' not 'python3', use 'dir' not 'ls', use '(echo a & echo b) | python script.py' for stdin piping. Avoid 'echo -e'.
shell_exec.returns: { status, exit_code, stdout, stderr } or { status, error }
